title: codiaeum chrysosticton
intro: Codiaeum chrysosticton, also known as the golden croton or fire croton, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Australia and Southeast Asia and is valued for its vibrant foliage and potential medicinal properties. The plant contains anthraquinones, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and have been found to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for reducing fever and alleviating pain. Codiaeum chrysosticton can be prepared into various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
